items.title: Team Config Broadcast via Git
items.description: Sync a shared team config from a git repo (e.g. `github.com/org/ai-config`) to all harnesses on every developer's machine. When the team lead updates the shared repo, every developer's next sync pulls those changes automatically. Eliminates the #ai-tools Slack channel noise of 'hey did you update your AGENTS.md to include the new rule about...'

items.title: Per-Branch Sync Profiles
items.description: Automatically apply different sync profiles when switching git branches. Feature branches get relaxed rules, main/release branches get strict lint rules synced everywhere. Hooks into git's post-checkout. Solves the frustration of forgetting to tighten AI tool configs before merging to main.

items.title: Harness Capability Advisor
items.description: Before syncing, analyze your Claude Code config and tell you exactly what WILL and WON'T transfer to each target, with specific reasons and workarounds. 'Your /deploy-prod skill uses bash heredocs — Aider will drop the formatting, here's an equivalent.' Prevents the silent degradation where synced configs technically exist but behave differently.

items.title: Config Drift Alerts
items.description: Detect when a target harness config has been manually edited outside of HarnessSync and notify the user. 'Your Cursor rules were modified 2 hours ago by someone (or something) other than HarnessSync — sync will overwrite these changes. Review first?' Prevents accidental overwrite of local harness-specific tweaks.

items.title: Rule Portability Score
items.description: Score each rule/skill/agent in your Claude Code config on how well it will translate across all configured targets (0-100). Aggregate into a 'Harness Portability Score' for the whole project. Helps users understand ROI of their config investment and prioritize which rules to write in portable vs. CC-specific ways.

items.title: MCP Server Compatibility Bridge
items.description: When a target harness doesn't support MCP natively, generate a stub script that approximates the MCP server's behavior using the harness's native tool-calling or slash-command system. For example, bridge a custom database MCP tool to Aider as a `/db-query` shell command wrapper. Unlocks MCP investment for harnesses without native support.

items.title: Config Templating with Variables
items.description: Support `{{project_name}}`, `{{team_name}}`, `{{git_remote}}` variables in CLAUDE.md rules that get resolved differently for each target harness at sync time. A rule that says 'You are working on {{project_name}}' becomes accurate across all harnesses without manual editing. Eliminates stale project names in AI rules.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Discovery
items.description: Periodically scan for newly installed AI coding tools and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Detect installation of new tools via PATH changes, .config directories, or known binary names. 'We detected Amp CLI was installed — add it as a sync target?' Eliminates the problem of new tools starting with a blank slate.

items.title: Harness-Agnostic Rule DSL
items.description: Introduce a lightweight declarative rule format (e.g. YAML frontmatter in CLAUDE.md sections) that HarnessSync uses to generate idiomatic config for each harness rather than copying raw markdown. A rule tagged `type: lint-gate` becomes a Cursor .mdc rule, a Gemini tool constraint, and an Aider convention automatically, each in the native idiom.

items.title: Community Config Registry
items.description: A browsable registry of community-contributed HarnessSync profiles (e.g. 'TypeScript monorepo', 'Django API', 'Machine Learning research'). Install a profile with one command and get a pre-populated CLAUDE.md plus all adapter configs. Solves the blank-slate problem for new projects and surfaces best practices from the community.

items.title: AI-Assisted Rule Generation
items.description: Describe in plain English what you want your AI coding tools to do or avoid, and HarnessSync generates the appropriate CLAUDE.md section plus all target-native equivalents. 'Never suggest console.log for debugging' → writes the CC rule, Codex AGENTS.md entry, Cursor .mdc, and Gemini constraint automatically. Removes the cold-start problem of not knowing how to write good AI rules.

items.title: Scheduled Background Sync
items.description: Set up a cron-style sync schedule so that even if users forget to run `/sync`, their harnesses stay current. Show last-sync timestamps in `/sync-status`. With a configurable staleness threshold that warns ('Your Gemini config is 4 days behind Claude Code') in the Claude Code session banner.

items.title: CI/CD Pipeline Sync Action
items.description: A GitHub Action / GitLab CI step that runs HarnessSync in a pipeline to ensure team repositories always have up-to-date harness configs checked in. Opens a PR when drift is detected. Solves the team-scale problem where individual devs' tools drift from the agreed team config stored in the repo.

items.title: Sync Conflict Resolution Wizard
items.description: When reverse sync or team sync detects conflicting rules between Claude Code and a target, launch an interactive side-by-side comparison to let users choose which version wins or merge them. Prevents silent data loss during bidirectional sync scenarios and gives users control over their config destiny.

items.title: Cross-Harness Skill Smoke Tests
items.description: After syncing a skill to a target harness, automatically run a simple prompt through that harness's CLI to verify the skill was loaded correctly and produces a sensible response. Report pass/fail per skill per harness. Catches silent sync failures where the file exists but the harness ignores it due to format errors.

items.title: Rule Deduplication and Consolidation
items.description: Scan all rules across all synced harness targets and identify semantic duplicates or contradictions. 'Rule 3 in CLAUDE.md and Rule 7 both say to avoid global state, but in different words — consolidate?' Keeps the source of truth clean and prevents AI tools from receiving redundant or contradictory guidance.

items.title: Task-to-Harness Recommendation
items.description: Based on the current task (e.g. refactoring, writing tests, exploring a codebase), recommend which configured harness is best suited for the job based on your sync profile and each harness's known strengths. 'For large-scale refactoring across 50 files, Aider's whole-repo mode tends to outperform Claude Code here.' Helps users get maximum value from their harness portfolio.

items.title: Primary Harness Migration Wizard
items.description: A guided wizard to help users migrate FROM another harness (e.g. Cursor) TO Claude Code as their primary, with HarnessSync as the sync layer going forward. Analyzes existing target configs, maps them to CLAUDE.md structure, and produces a migration report with manual steps for anything that can't auto-convert.

items.title: Project Type Auto-Profiles
items.description: Detect project type from the codebase (Next.js, Django, Rust CLI, etc.) and suggest or auto-apply a HarnessSync profile optimized for that stack — including harness-specific rules, appropriate MCP servers, and relevant skills. Removes the burden of knowing what to configure for each new project.

items.title: Visual Sync Timeline and Rollback
items.description: A time-ordered visual history of every sync event with clickable rollback to any prior state per target. Show what changed in each sync, who triggered it, and how long it took. The `/sync-rollback` command exists but make it a first-class navigable timeline, not just a last-state undo.

items.title: Live MCP Reachability Monitor
items.description: Continuously monitor configured MCP servers and show their health status in the sync dashboard. Alert when a server that's configured in Claude Code and synced to other harnesses goes offline. 'Your Postgres MCP server has been unreachable for 20 minutes — Codex and Gemini configs still reference it.' Prevents users from debugging mysterious harness failures.

items.title: Semantic Diff Explanations
items.description: When showing sync diffs, go beyond line-level changes to explain the semantic meaning. 'You added a rule that restricts file deletion — this will prevent Aider from auto-removing test fixtures.' Uses the AI to interpret what config changes actually mean in practice, not just what bytes changed.

items.title: Harness Warmup / Onboarding Scripts
items.description: Generate a one-shot script that bootstraps any target harness from zero to fully configured — installs the tool if missing, authenticates, applies the synced config, and runs a smoke test. Share this script so team members can onboard to any harness in one command. Eliminates the 'it works on my machine' problem for AI tool configs.

items.title: Config Snapshot Versioning
items.description: Tag a snapshot of the current sync state as a named version (e.g. `v1.0-pre-launch-freeze`) and allow any target to be pinned to that version regardless of future changes. Useful for release branches that need stable AI tool configs while the main branch evolves. Like package.lock but for your AI coding setup.

items.title: Environment-Aware Sync (dev/staging/prod)
items.description: Define environment-specific sync rules so harnesses configured in a prod-adjacent context get stricter rules (no code deletion, require tests) while dev environments get relaxed rules. Sync profiles that map to git environment context automatically. Prevents AI tools from taking risky actions in the wrong context.

items.title: Claude Code Plugin Sync to Other Harnesses
items.description: Sync not just configs but entire plugin functionality — translate Claude Code plugin commands/skills into native equivalents for target harnesses. A Claude Code plugin that adds `/db-migrate` becomes an Aider custom command and a Gemini skill automatically. Multiplies the value of every plugin the user installs.

items.title: Sync Impact Predictor
items.description: Before syncing, estimate the behavioral impact on each target harness — not just file changes but 'this new rule will cause Codex to reject ~15% of its current tool use patterns based on your recent history.' Connects config changes to likely behavior changes so users understand consequences before committing.

items.title: Cross-Harness Token Cost Tracker
items.description: Aggregate token usage and cost estimates across all configured harnesses and display a unified view. Show which harnesses are most expensive, which synced skills or agents drive the most tokens, and trends over time. Helps users optimize their AI tool spending across their whole harness portfolio.

items.title: Offline Sync Queue with Replay
items.description: When a target harness account or path is unavailable (remote machine offline, VPN down), queue sync operations locally and replay them when connectivity is restored. Show queued operations in `/sync-status`. Prevents config drift for users who work across multiple machines or with remote dev environments.

items.title: Rule Effectiveness Feedback Loop
items.description: Let users mark synced rules as 'working well' or 'not helping' directly from their harness sessions, with feedback surfaced in HarnessSync. Aggregate this to show which rules have positive signal across harnesses and which to prune. Closes the loop between rule authoring and actual AI behavior improvement.

items.title: Harness Parity Gap Report
items.description: A periodic (weekly/monthly) report showing the cumulative capability gap between Claude Code and each target harness — what percentage of your CC investment has been successfully replicated, what's been approximated, and what's been lost. Helps users make informed decisions about which harnesses are worth maintaining as sync targets.

items.title: Webhook-Triggered Sync
items.description: Expose a local webhook endpoint that external systems can POST to in order to trigger a sync. Allows CI/CD pipelines, Slack bots, or other automation to push new configs to all harnesses without the user being present. Enables org-level config management at scale.

items.title: Secret and Credential Scrubber
items.description: Before syncing, scan all content for potential secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords in env var examples) and either redact them with placeholders or block the sync with a specific warning. Prevents the embarrassing (and dangerous) scenario of accidentally syncing a CLAUDE.md containing a real API key to a shared repository or harness config.

items.title: Natural Language Sync Query
items.description: Ask questions about your sync state in plain English within the Claude Code session. 'Which of my rules aren't supported by Aider?' / 'When did my Gemini config last change and what was different?' / 'Show me everything that differs between my Codex and OpenCode configs.' Makes the sync state queryable without memorizing command syntax.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.
